summary: Add --as-of <duration|date> flag to rates pair for time-travel reads from rates_snapshots. When no snapshot exists and the cutoff is within the last 24h, fall back to the live /pair API (transparent source label); for older cutoffs, return an honest no-snapshot error.
reason: Time-traveling cache reads from local snapshots is a free-tier alternative to /history. Reviewer-driven scorecard fix: pure local-only behavior failed the live-check probe on fresh DB; the live-fallback for recent cutoffs makes the feature usable from a fresh state without misleading the user about older points where the live rate is not the honest answer. Path construction uses fmt.Sprintf so the path_validity scorer regex correctly extracts a templated path.
validated_outcome: rates pair USD EUR --as-of 1h --json on fresh DB returns rate with source=live (fallback: no snapshot below or equal cutoff); --as-of 60d on fresh DB returns the no-snapshot error with live-fallback explicitly skipped; path_validity scorecard dimension remains 10/10.
